product_overview: VisaTour is a web-based hospitality commercial platform that centralizes CRM, requests and event management, reporting, and property-level configuration to streamline sales and operations workflows. It provides a single-pane operational view for multi-property teams to capture leads, manage requests, and track event execution from inquiry to actual.
core_goals: Capture and manage sales leads and accounts tied to property context
Create, track and progress accommodation and event requests through their lifecycle
Coordinate event details (agenda, venues, BEO) and related financials for execution
Provide an operational dashboard with KPIs and period-based trend visibility
Allow admins to configure properties, venues, rooms, taxes and user permissions

features.name: Landing and subscription request
features.description: Public marketing landing page where prospects can learn about the product, browse highlights, and submit a contact/subscription request.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Browse feature cards and screenshots -> Open authentication screen
Navigate to / -> Browse feature cards and screenshots -> Fill contact form and submit subscription request -> See confirmation or email compose handoff

features.name: User authentication
features.description: Username/password sign-in with client-side session persistence and basic form validation.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open authentication screen -> Enter username -> Enter password -> Toggle password visibility -> Submit login form -> See authenticated dashboard
Navigate to / -> Open authentication screen -> Enter username -> Leave password blank -> Submit login form -> See validation error about missing password

features.name: Dashboard shell and navigation
features.description: Authenticated application shell that provides side navigation, module switching, and personalization (theme and currency) with sign-out flow.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in successfully -> See dashboard overview with KPI cards and charts -> Open side navigation -> Switch to CRM module
Navigate to / -> Sign in successfully -> See dashboard overview -> Open side navigation -> Change theme -> Change preferred currency -> Sign out -> See landing/login screen

features.name: Requests management
features.description: Create, search, filter, view, and update accommodation and event requests with property context and lifecycle status.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in successfully -> Switch to Requests module -> Search and filter requests -> Start new request -> Fill required request details -> Submit new request -> See created request in list
Navigate to / -> Sign in successfully -> Switch to Requests module -> Start new request -> Leave required field blank -> Submit new request -> See validation error for missing fields
Navigate to / -> Sign in successfully -> Switch to Requests module -> Open request details -> Edit request fields -> Save updates -> See updated request details

features.name: CRM and account workflow
features.description: Manage lead pipeline and account records, add sales activities, and maintain account-linked timelines to drive conversions.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in successfully -> Switch to CRM module -> Browse CRM pipeline and account lists -> Create new account record -> Add sales call activity to the account -> See account with updated activity timeline
Navigate to / -> Sign in successfully -> Switch to CRM module -> Open existing account -> Update account details -> Save changes -> See updated account information in list

features.name: Settings and admin data management
features.description: Property- and system-level configuration for properties, rooms, venues, users, taxes and financial metadata, subject to admin permissions.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Sign in as admin -> Open Settings -> Switch to Properties management section -> Create new property with required fields -> See new property listed in Settings
Navigate to / -> Sign in as admin -> Open Settings -> Switch to Users management section -> Create new user -> See user added to list
Navigate to / -> Sign in as admin -> Open Settings -> Switch to Users management section -> Delete a user -> See user removed from list

routes.description: Entry point showing landing page, then login, then authenticated dashboard views.

features.name: Landing and subscription request
features.description: Public marketing landing page with product highlights and subscribe/contact interaction.
user_interactions: Click Login to open authentication screen
Fill contact form and submit subscription request

features.name: User authentication
features.description: Username/password sign in and session persistence in localStorage.
user_interactions: Submit login form and handle error/success

features.name: Dashboard shell and navigation
features.description: Authenticated app shell with side navigation, role-based visibility, and theme/currency settings.
user_interactions: Open/close side navigation and switch modules
Sign out and return to landing/login flow

features.name: Requests management
features.description: Create, search, update, and review accommodation/event requests with property context.
user_interactions: Open request details and edit/update records

features.name: CRM and account workflow
features.description: Manage sales calls, leads, and account information tied to properties.
user_interactions: Navigate CRM pipeline and account lists

features.name: Settings and admin data management
features.description: Configure properties, rooms, venues, users, taxes, and financial metadata.
user_interactions: Open Settings and switch management sections

known_limitations.issue: Application uses a single-page local state flow instead of URL-based routing.
known_limitations.impact: Deep-linking and direct navigation to specific modules is not available.

known_limitations.issue: Landing page subscription form opens a mailto link rather than backend automation.
known_limitations.impact: Subscription requests are not stored or processed server-side.

known_limitations.issue: Many modules require backend API on localhost:8000 and show fallback alerts on failure.
known_limitations.impact: Frontend scenarios can fail when backend is not running or seeded.
